gaps.title: Live ChatGPT cognition is not operationally present
gaps.current_reality: Semantic proposal and semantic contract are local deterministic derivations.
gaps.risk: Operators may infer live ChatGPT semantic cognition from ChatGPT-facing labels.
gaps.required_truth_label: LOCAL DETERMINISTIC NORMALIZATION; NON-AUTHORITATIVE; SEMANTIC ONLY

gaps.title: Live ChatGPT interpretation is not present on return
gaps.current_reality: Governed return is generated by Python/JS as an operator summary; it is not sent to live ChatGPT.
gaps.risk: ChatGPT-facing return wording can imply model interpretation that does not occur.
gaps.required_truth_label: GOVERNED OPERATOR RETURN; NOT LIVE CHATGPT INTERPRETATION

gaps.title: Durable replay backend is absent from the native minimal bridge
gaps.current_reality: Replay events are deterministic records returned in-memory and inside exported/imported artifacts; event records mark durable_persistence false.
gaps.risk: Replay visibility can be mistaken for durable replay persistence.

gaps.title: Sidepanel replay session is ephemeral
gaps.current_reality: lifecycleEntries and replaySessionEntries are in-memory arrays with no localStorage, sessionStorage, indexedDB, chrome.storage, or durable file backend.
gaps.risk: Session cards may look replayable after reload when they are not.

gaps.title: Post-execution verification is structural only
gaps.current_reality: Result validation checks schema, lineage, session/proposal linkage, status enum, and forbidden authority flags; it does not verify semantic correctness of Codex output.
gaps.risk: A structurally valid result may be read as semantically correct.
gaps.required_truth_label: STRUCTURAL VERIFICATION ONLY; semantic correctness is not verified

gaps.title: Canonical Python result artifact import verifies integrity only
gaps.current_reality: Sidepanel verifies result artifact schema and hash, then renders it. Import does not prove semantic correctness, persistence, or approval.
gaps.risk: Canonical import can be mistaken for approval or semantic certification.

gaps.title: JS-only demo and mock paths remain present as legacy experimental surfaces
gaps.current_reality: Legacy sidepanel controls include mock execution and JS-only bridge paths distinct from the canonical Native Messaging path.
gaps.risk: Legacy controls can be confused with the canonical real Codex runtime if labels are not read carefully.

gaps.title: Codex execution is real only on the Native Messaging/Python path
gaps.current_reality: The canonical Python minimal bridge invokes subprocess.run(['codex', 'exec', bounded_prompt]) through the bounded provider wrapper; sidepanel-only demo paths do not.
gaps.risk: Operators may assume every rendered path invoked Codex.
gaps.required_truth_label: REAL_CODEX_EXECUTION only when canonical native Python runtime artifact or provider_result is present

gaps.title: Provider success is not semantic task completion proof
gaps.current_reality: Codex CLI return code and captured stdout/stderr are recorded; files_changed is not independently discovered by the minimal bridge and semantic completion is not verified.
gaps.risk: A COMPLETED status can be mistaken for validated implementation correctness.

gaps.title: Native host installation and Chrome Native Messaging registration are environmental, not guaranteed by repo code
gaps.current_reality: Service worker can call chrome.runtime.sendNativeMessage only if host registration and permissions exist in the operator environment.
gaps.risk: Repo presence of host code can be mistaken for installed operational transport.

gaps.title: Active ChatGPT bridge module is not the browser companion live ChatGPT path
gaps.current_reality: sapianta_bridge/active_chatgpt_bridge composes bounded components and defaults to deterministic_mock, but the audited browser companion path does not establish live ChatGPT operational presence.
gaps.risk: Module names can imply a current live ChatGPT integration that is not present in the cockpit/native path.

gaps.title: Observability can imply enforcement where only labels exist
gaps.current_reality: Observatory cards render authority, topology, and lifecycle summaries from current entry fields; cards themselves do not enforce runtime behavior.
gaps.risk: UI labels may be read as runtime authority rather than descriptions.
gaps.required_truth_label: UI_ONLY unless backed by service worker, native host, Python validation, provider boundary, or result_validation artifact
